data.title: Prudential Financial to redeem all outstanding subordinated notes due 2045
data.description:
• Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) plans to redeem all of the $1B outstanding aggregate principal amount of its 5.375% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2045.
  • The notes will be redeemed on May 15, 2025 at a price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the Notes, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the redemption date.

data.title: SocGen says fade the Nasdaq 100 rallies
data.description:
Growth stocks aren't out of the woods yet and investors should be wary of rallies, according to Societe Generale's global asset team.

Strategist Manish Kabra advises investors to fade rallies of the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) (NASDAQ:QQQ), adding "our bearish view on NDX drove us to underweight US and Global Equities early on but the 'shock' may keep the UW for longer."

Tech and consumer stocks, a "big part of NDX," are at center stage as about 40% of the supply chains of those sectors are exposed to Asia, Kabra added.

For the broader market, the SocGen Cycle Indicators are signaling "muddle through" status for the S&P 500 (SP500) (NYSEARCA:SPY) (NYSEARCA:IVV) (NYSEARCA:VOO), which means between 5,000 and 6,000 for the index.

But the cycle can shift on "extreme tariffs," which could lead to a "downturn" scenario with the index trading between 4,600 and 5,500.

The S&P has big technical support at its 200-week moving average around 4,677.

Fed rate cuts would come into play when 5y5y forward inflation expectations fall below 2%, Kabra said.

He noted three positives for the U.S.: "Private sector leverage is low and falling, Top-10% of US Earners have cycle-high savings and Corporate activities are not exuberant."

SocGen recommends going long Utilities (XLU), domestic Industrials (XLI) and Financials (XLF), as well as stocks linked to the domestic supply chain, reshoring and certain small-cap value stocks.

data.title: Citigroup Q1 earnings top consensus, helped by markets, investment banking
data.description:
Citigroup (NYSE:C) stock gained 1.7% in Tuesday premarket trading after the bank reaffirmed 2025 guidance and its Q1 earnings and revenue beat consensus estimates, with strong performance in its markets and investment banking businesses.

The bank kept its 2025 guidance unchanged, subject to macro and market conditions. It expects net interest income, ex-markets, up 2%-3% from 2024. Full-year revenue is expected to be ~$83.1B-$84.1B compared with its prior view of $83.5B-$84.5B, vs. the average analyst estimate of $83.6B. Expenses for the year are projected to be "slightly below" $53.4B.

"With net income of $4.1B we delivered a strong quarter, marked by continued momentum, positive operating leverage and improved returns in each of our five businesses, CEO Jane Fraser said.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.96, topping the average analyst estimate of $1.84, jumped from $1.34 in Q4 2024 and $1.58 in last year’s Q1.

Revenue of $21.6B, exceeding the $21.3B consensus, rose from $19.5B in the previous quarter and $21.0B a year ago.

Q1 return on tangible common equity was 9.1%, up from 6.1% in Q4.

Q1 net credit losses totaled $2.46B vs. $2.24B in Q4 and $2.30B in last year’s Q1.

Q1 net interest income of $14.0B, missing the Visible Alpha consensus of $14.6B, increased from $13.7B in the previous quarter and $13.5B a year ago.

Total operating expenses grew to $13.4B from $13.1B in Q4 and declined from $14.1B in Q1 2024.

Loans stood at $702B at March 31, 2025, up from $694B at Dec. 31, 2024. Deposits rose to $1.36T at the end of the quarter from $1.28T at the end of Q4.

Revenue by segment:

Services total revenue dipped 5% Q/Q and increased 3% Y/Y to $4.89B. Treasury and Trade Solutions revenue of $3.64B fell 7% Q/Q and rose 4% Y/Y. Securities Services revenue of $1.25B rose 2% Q/Q and was flat Y/Y.

Markets revenue jumped 31% Q/Q and 12% Y/Y to $5.99B. Fixed income markets revenue of $4.48B climbed 29% Q/Q and 8% Y/Y and equity markets revenue of $1.51B rose 31% Q/Q and 12% Y/Y. 

Banking revenue of $1.94B surged 55% Q/Q and rose 5% Y/Y, including investment banking revenue of $1.04B, which gained 12% Q/Q and 12% Y/Y, and corporate lending revenue of $903M, up 180% Q/Q and down 1% Y/Y.

U.S. Personal Banking revenue of $5.23B rose 2% both Q/Q and Y/Y. Retail services revenue of $1.68B dipped 4% Q/Q and 11% Y/Y. Branded cards revenue of $2.89B increased 3% Q/Q and 9% Y/Y. Retail banking revenue of $661M grew 10% Q/Q and 17% Y/Y.

Wealth revenue of $2.10B increased 1% Q/Q and 24% Y/Y. Citigold revenue of $1.16B rose 1% Q/Q and 24% Y/Y. Private bank revenue of $664M climbed 13% Q/Q and 16% Y/Y.

Conference call at 11:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Citigroup GAAP EPS of $1.96 beats by $0.12, revenue of $21.6B beats by $310M

data.title: Lisata Therapeutics announces research license with Catalent
data.description:
• Lisata Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LSTA) said on Tuesday that the company has entered into a research license with Catalent to evaluate the efficacy of Lisata's iRGD cyclic peptide product candidate, certepetide, as a payload used in the context of Catalent’s SMARTag antibody-drug conjugate dual-payload technology platform for the treatment of difficult-to-treat-diseases, including advanced solid tumors.   
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Catalent will assume full responsibility for all research and development expenses and Lisata will provide consulting support. 
  • Further, Lisata will receive an upfront payment with the possibility of future considerations contingent upon the results of the preclinical evaluation.
  • Shares of Lisata Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LSTA) were up more than 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Hedge fund Qube is short on Trump Media - Reuters
data.description:
Hedge fund Qube Research & Technologies has disclosed a nearly $105M short position in Trump Media & Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT), Reuters reported on Monday, citing a filing with Germany's federal Gazette Bundesanzeiger.

Qube's disclosed position in the Truth Social parent translates to 2.53% of the total free float of DJT stock, which is 53% owned by U.S. President Donald Trump, the report said.

Qube said in an e-mail to Reuters that its positions are driven by a quantitative model and "do not reflect a specific view on the fundamental of the company."

DJT is down nearly 29% in the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 is up 3.2%. The company has a market cap of $4.17B, and the short interest position is 4.86%, Seeking Alpha data shows.

DJT is rated "sell" by Seeking Alpha analysts on average.

data.title: Janover purchases $10.5M of Solana as part of new treasury strategy
data.description:
• Janover (NASDAQ:JNVR) has announced the purchase of 80,567 Solana valued at ~$10.5M - the third execution under its newly adopted digital asset treasury strategy.
  • This purchase brings Janover’s total Solana holdings to 163,651.7, valued at ~$21.2M – inclusive of staking rewards.
  • Total Shares Outstanding are ~1.5M; SOL per Share: 0.11, valued at $14.47/share.
  • The Co. will begin staking its newly acquired SOL immediately, generating revenue while supporting the Solana network.

data.title: SA Charts: Crypto miners see rise in production tallies in March
data.description:
The biggest Bitcoin miners by market capitalization saw their production tallies rise in March, taking a respite from the sequential dips in the previous months.

Meanwhile, the price of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell about 2.1% over March, as the world's largest cryptocurrency see-sawed on tariff-related news as President Donald Trump's administration set its trade war agenda. 

Adding to the inflation woes, late in March, the Fed's preferred inflation measure, the core PCE index, increased in February to +0.4% M/M, hotter than the consensus estimate and the highest rate since January 2024.

Of the lot, miners MARA Holdings (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT), CleanSpark (CLSK), IREN Limited (IREN), Core Scientific (CORZ), Cipher Mining (CIFR), Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR), Hut 8 (HUT), and Bitfarms (BITF) saw a sequential increase in their monthly production numbers.

 

 

Looking at the March performance for this group of miners, the group produced 3,540 bitcoins during the month, which is 17.1% more than the February production.

Since the start of the year, bitcoin has fallen about 9.5%, underperforming the S&P 500 index, which has lost about 7.5% during the same period.

data.title: Lifeward launches sales of ReWalk 7 personal exoskeleton in U.S.
data.description:
• Lifeward (NASDAQ:LFWD) on Tuesday announced the U.S. national launch of the ReWalk 7 Personal Exoskeleton, the next generation of its personal exoskeleton technology. 
  • The company will now begin sales of ReWalk 7, which includes new and enhanced features such as cloud connectivity and customizable walking speeds. 
  • As part of the FDA clearance process, Lifeward worked with more than two dozen end users and physical therapists, respectively, to gather critical feedback on the impact of the device in both the rehabilitative and real-world settings.
  • The ReWalk 7 is now available for purchase in the U.S. for personal use, as well as in the clinical setting.
  • Lifeward (NASDAQ:LFWD) shares were up 2.78% in premarket trading.
